tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated creatinine and chloride, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, glycosuria, and ketonuria with abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings indicate possible acute kidney dysfunction and metabolic acidosis, with urinary abnormalities suggestive of renal tubular dysfunction or possible urinary tract pathology. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying causes, including possible infection or acute metabolic disturbance.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
